next_steps: Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with transbronchial biopsy
Send BAL fluid for GMS stain, AFB smear and culture, fungal culture, and PCR for P. jirovecii and M. tuberculosis
Obtain serum cryptococcal antigen and histoplasma antigen
Maintain empirical coverage for P. jirovecii pneumonia with high-dose trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole and corticosteroids
Initiate fluid resuscitation and hemodynamic support for septic shock
Delay initiation of antiretroviral therapy to avoid immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) until initial infection is stabilized
Obtain blood cultures for bacteria and fungi
Proceed with urgent bronchoscopy and bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for microbiological and pathological analysis, including GMS, AFB, fungal cultures, and relevant PCR assays
Obtain serum (1,3)-beta-D-glucan assay, histoplasma antigen, and cryptococcal antigen
Initiate and maintain broad-spectrum empirical treatment for suspected opportunistic pathogens including P. jirovecii (high-dose TMP-SMX and corticosteroids)
Provide aggressive hemodynamic support and oxygenation management for septic shock
Delay initiation of antiretroviral therapy (ART) to minimize the risk of immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS)
Perform a thorough skin examination for lesions suggestive of disseminated fungal or mycobacterial infection or Kaposi sarcoma
Consider ophthalmologic evaluation for CMV retinitis as a marker for systemic disease
